title_tesim: Harry Owens and daughter Leilani in publicity still for Cocoanut Grove
caption_tesim: Text from back of photograph: Sweet Leilani Comes to Hollywood - The "Academy Award Baby" is what Hollywood calls this entrancing little miss, whose lullaby, "Sweet Leilani," has been nominated for a Motion Picture Academy award as the best song of 1937. Here are Leilani, herself, and her daddy, Harry Owens, the music-writing maestro. They were brought to Hollywood by Paramount especially for the big musical show "Cocoanut Grove," for which Owens has written several new numbers an din which he appears as an actor and as leader of his own Royal Hawaiian Orchestra.
repository_tesim: University of California, Los Angeles. Library. Performing Arts Special Collections

title_tesim: Harry Owens and Harold Lloyd on the set of Cocoanut Grove
caption_tesim: Text from back of photograph: Composer Autographs New Hit - Harry Owens, whose "Sweet Leilani" won the Motion Picture Academy award as the best film song of 1937, autographs his new hit tune, "Cocoanut Grove," for Harold Lloyd. Owens and his Royal Hawaiian Orchestra from Honolulu were specifically imported by Paramount to appear in the filmusical, "Cocoanut Grove," for which the maestro has written the music.
repository_tesim: University of California, Los Angeles. Library. Performing Arts Special Collections
description_tesim: Harry Owens (left) and Harold Lloyd on the set of "Cocoanut Grove."

caption_tesim: Text from back of photograph: Capes - are back in various versions for Hollywood's early summer fashion trend. Dorothy Howe in Paramount's "Cocoanut Grove" wears a daytime dress of brown jersey with an interesting belt of shiny gold kid. The neckline is collarless but cut in a shallow, scalloped "V" which is repeated at the hem of the dress. The sleeves are long and bloused below the elbow over a tight cuff. An unlined, circular cape of beige wool crepe fastens at one side of the neckline and is banded in mink. Brown accessories and a muff of mink complete the costume.
repository_tesim: University of California, Los Angeles. Library. Performing Arts Special Collections
